content:
# Final Review Arbiter
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Accept `fr-7-fbca41a9`: current backfill misclassifies legacy populated `bead_id` values as implicit-slot matches, but repo history shows pre-upgrade `plan.json` did not auto-generate implicit bead IDs, so those populated legacy IDs should be treated as explicit and covered by a title-drift regression test.

## Rulings

- `fr-7-fbca41a9`: Accept
  The amendment identifies a real legacy-compatibility defect. In current code, `backfill_legacy_explicit_bead_flags` leaves `explicit_id` unset when a legacy `bead_id` matches the implicit slot (`implicit_slot_matches`), and `proposal_matches_bead_id` later rejects all ID-based matching unless `explicit_id == Some(true)`. That means a legacy entry like `bead-1` or `ms-alpha.bead-1` can only resolve through title fallback; if the live bead title changes, `resolve_bead_plan` drops to the unconfirmed path and loses metadata such as `flow_override` and membership confirmation. The deciding evidence is the pre-upgrade behavior: before `explicit_id` existed, legacy `plan.json` was rendered by directly serializing the bundle rather than auto-filling implicit bead IDs, so a populated legacy `bead_id` was planner-authored explicit identity, not an automatically synthesized slot marker. The existing legacy test only covers the unchanged-title case, so a regression test with `bead_id: "bead-1"`, no `explicit_id`, and a renamed live bead is warranted.
